1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# scripts/benchmark/benchmark — operator-facing benchmark driver.
3	#
4	# Replaces `fiz-bench sweep` / `fiz-bench matrix` execution. Pure shell:
5	# yq + jq + sha256sum + docker. No Go bridge, no Python.
6	#
7	# Bead fizeau-fe87a3ac: skeleton + listing subcommands + --plan mode +
8	# preflight rebuild gate. The cell execution loop (resume, retry, signal
9	# handling, concurrency, budget) lands in a follow-up bead.
10	#
11	# Operator contract:
12	#   ./benchmark profiles
13	#   ./benchmark bench-sets
14	#   ./benchmark harness-adapters
15	#   ./benchmark task-executors
16	#   ./benchmark validate                            # placeholder; see PR C
17	#   ./benchmark preflight                           # rebuild harbor-runner if sha drifted
18	#   ./benchmark --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] --plan
19	#   ./benchmark --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...]   # run path; PR A4
20	#
21	# Rules:
22	#   1. --plan is pure: no file writes, no Docker pulls/builds, no
23	#      benchmark-results/ creation. Prints one matrix line per cell.
24	#   2. --profile and --bench-set are both required for --plan and run.
25	#   3. preflight is the only command that touches Docker.
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
30	PROFILES_DIR="${PROFILES_DIR:-${SCRIPT_DIR}/profiles}"
31	BENCH_SETS_DIR="${BENCH_SETS_DIR:-${SCRIPT_DIR}/bench-sets}"
32	HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR="${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR:-${SCRIPT_DIR}/harness-adapters}"
33	TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR="${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR:-${SCRIPT_DIR}/task-executors}"
34	HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/build-harbor-runner.sh"
35	HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG="${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG:-fizeau-harbor-runner:latest}"
36	DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT="${DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT:-bench/results/fiz-tools-v1}"
37	BENCH_TASKS_DIR_DEFAULT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/external/terminal-bench-2/tasks"
38	BENCH_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS="${BENCH_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS:-4}"
39	BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE="${BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE:-1}"
40	
41	die() { printf 'benchmark: %s\n' "$*" >&2; exit 1; }
42	warn() { printf 'benchmark: %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
43	
44	require_tool() {
45	  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "required tool not found: $1"
46	}
47	
48	usage() {
49	  cat >&2 <<EOF
50	Usage:
51	  ./benchmark profiles
52	  ./benchmark bench-sets
53	  ./benchmark harness-adapters
54	  ./benchmark task-executors
55	  ./benchmark validate
56	  ./benchmark preflight
57	  ./benchmark --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] [--plan] [--out DIR]
58	              [--force-rerun] [--retry-invalid] [--reps N]
59	
60	Flags (run mode):
61	  --profile P[,P2,...]   Comma-separated profile IDs (required)
62	  --bench-set B[,B2,...] Comma-separated bench-set IDs (required)
63	  --plan                 Validate + print matrix; pure, no side effects
64	  --out DIR              Output root (default: ${DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT}); ignored under --plan
65	  --force-rerun          Always mint reps fresh cells; ignore terminal report.json
66	  --retry-invalid        Re-run cells whose final_status is invalid (non-empty
67	                         invalid_class) or whose cell-state.json is orphan
68	                         (mid-run crash). New cell links via attempt_of; the
69	                         prior cell is back-written with superseded_by.
70	  --reps N               Override bench-set default_reps for every cell
71	EOF
72	}
73	
74	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
75	# Listing subcommands
76	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77	
78	cmd_profiles() {
79	  local f
80	  local entries=()
81	  shopt -s nullglob
82	  for f in "${PROFILES_DIR}"/*.yaml "${PROFILES_DIR}"/*.yml; do
83	    entries+=("$(basename "${f%.*}")")
84	  done
85	  shopt -u nullglob
86	  if [[ ${#entries[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
87	    warn "no profiles found in ${PROFILES_DIR}"
88	    return 0
89	  fi
90	  printf '%s\n' "${entries[@]}" | LC_ALL=C sort -u
91	}
92	
93	cmd_bench_sets() {
94	  local f
95	  local entries=()
96	  shopt -s nullglob
97	  for f in "${BENCH_SETS_DIR}"/*.yaml "${BENCH_SETS_DIR}"/*.yml; do
98	    entries+=("$(basename "${f%.*}")")
99	  done
100	  shopt -u nullglob
101	  if [[ ${#entries[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
102	    warn "no bench-sets found in ${BENCH_SETS_DIR}"
103	    return 0
104	  fi
105	  printf '%s\n' "${entries[@]}" | LC_ALL=C sort -u
106	}
107	
108	# list_adapter_dir prints one "<name>\t<summary>" line per executable file.
109	# Files without a `# SUMMARY:` line on line 2 print "(no summary)" so the
110	# omission is visible. CONTRACT.md and other non-executables are skipped.
111	list_adapter_dir() {
112	  local dir="$1"
113	  [[ -d "${dir}" ]] || { warn "directory missing: ${dir}"; return 0; }
114	  local f name summary
115	  local rows=()
116	  shopt -s nullglob dotglob
117	  for f in "${dir}"/*; do
118	    [[ -f "${f}" && -x "${f}" ]] || continue
119	    name="$(basename "${f}")"
120	    summary="$(sed -n '2{/^# SUMMARY:[[:space:]]*/{s///;p;};q;}' "${f}")"
121	    [[ -n "${summary}" ]] || summary="(no summary)"
122	    rows+=("$(printf '%s\t%s' "${name}" "${summary}")")
123	  done
124	  shopt -u nullglob dotglob
125	  if [[ ${#rows[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
126	    warn "no adapters found in ${dir}"
127	    return 0
128	  fi
129	  printf '%s\n' "${rows[@]}" | LC_ALL=C sort -u
130	}
131	
132	cmd_harness_adapters() { list_adapter_dir "${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR}"; }
133	cmd_task_executors() { list_adapter_dir "${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR}"; }
134	
135	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
136	# Matrix expansion (yq -o=json + jq)
137	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
138	
139	resolve_profile_file() {
140	  local id="$1" f
141	  for f in "${PROFILES_DIR}/${id}.yaml" "${PROFILES_DIR}/${id}.yml"; do
142	    if [[ -f "${f}" ]]; then printf '%s\n' "${f}"; return 0; fi
143	  done
144	  die "profile not found: ${id} (looked in ${PROFILES_DIR})"
145	}
146	
147	resolve_bench_set_file() {
148	  local id="$1" f
149	  for f in "${BENCH_SETS_DIR}/${id}.yaml" "${BENCH_SETS_DIR}/${id}.yml"; do
150	    if [[ -f "${f}" ]]; then printf '%s\n' "${f}"; return 0; fi
151	  done
152	  die "bench-set not found: ${id} (looked in ${BENCH_SETS_DIR})"
153	}
154	
155	# bench_set_json emits the bench-set as JSON.
156	bench_set_json() {
157	  local file="$1"
158	  yq -o=json '.' "${file}"
159	}
160	
161	# resolve_tasks emits one task id per line for a bench-set.
162	# Handles three shapes:
163	#   tasks: [id, id, ...]         inline list
164	#   tasks: all                   defer to harness dataset; emit "*all*"
165	#   tasks_from: <path>           load tasks[].id from a sibling YAML
166	resolve_tasks() {
167	  local bs_file="$1"
168	  local bs_json tasks_kind tasks_from
169	  bs_json="$(bench_set_json "${bs_file}")"
170	
171	  tasks_kind="$(jq -r '
172	    if (.tasks // null) == null then "none"
173	    elif (.tasks | type) == "array" then "list"
174	    elif (.tasks | type) == "string" then ("string:" + .tasks)
175	    else "other"
176	    end' <<<"${bs_json}")"
177	
178	  tasks_from="$(jq -r '.tasks_from // ""' <<<"${bs_json}")"
179	
180	  if [[ "${tasks_kind}" == "list" ]]; then
181	    jq -r '.tasks[]' <<<"${bs_json}"
182	    return 0
183	  fi
184	  if [[ "${tasks_kind}" == "string:all" ]]; then
185	    # Defer to the harness dataset. The plan path emits a sentinel so
186	    # operators see the contract; the run path (PR A4) expands at execution.
187	    printf '*all*\n'
188	    return 0
189	  fi
190	  if [[ -n "${tasks_from}" ]]; then
191	    local resolved
192	    resolved="$(cd "$(dirname "${bs_file}")" && cd "$(dirname "${tasks_from}")" && pwd)/$(basename "${tasks_from}")"
193	    [[ -f "${resolved}" ]] || die "tasks_from path not found: ${tasks_from} (resolved to ${resolved})"
194	    yq -o=json '.' "${resolved}" \
195	      | jq -r '
196	          if (.tasks | type) == "array" then
197	            .tasks[] | (if type == "object" then .id else . end)
198	          else
199	            empty
200	          end'
201	    return 0
202	  fi
203	  die "bench-set ${bs_file}: no .tasks list, .tasks: all, or .tasks_from set"
204	}

239	# print_plan emits one line per (profile, task, rep) cell. Pure: no writes.
240	print_plan() {
241	  local profiles_csv="$1" bench_sets_csv="$2"
242	  IFS=',' read -r -a profile_ids <<<"${profiles_csv}"
243	  IFS=',' read -r -a bench_set_ids <<<"${bench_sets_csv}"
244	
245	  local profile_id bs_id bs_file framework dataset reps task_executor tasks
246	  local rep i n
247	
248	  for profile_id in "${profile_ids[@]}"; do
249	    [[ -n "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
250	    resolve_profile_file "${profile_id}" >/dev/null  # existence check
251	    for bs_id in "${bench_set_ids[@]}"; do
252	      [[ -n "${bs_id}" ]] || continue
253	      bs_file="$(resolve_bench_set_file "${bs_id}")"
254	      framework="$(bench_set_framework "${bs_file}")"
255	      dataset="$(bench_set_dataset "${bs_file}")"
256	      reps="$(bench_set_reps "${bs_file}")"
257	      task_executor="$(default_task_executor_for_framework "${framework}")"
258	      tasks="$(resolve_tasks "${bs_file}")"
259	      i=0
260	      while IFS= read -r task; do
261	        [[ -n "${task}" ]] || continue
262	        i=$((i + 1))
263	        for ((rep = 1; rep <= reps; rep++)); do
264	          n=$((rep))
265	          printf 'profile=%s\tbench_set=%s\tframework=%s\tdataset=%s\ttask=%s\trep=%d/%d\ttask_executor=%s\n' \
266	            "${profile_id}" "${bs_id}" "${framework}" "${dataset}" \
267	            "${task}" "${n}" "${reps}" "${task_executor}"
268	        done
269	      done <<<"${tasks}"
270	      [[ "${i}" -gt 0 ]] || die "bench-set ${bs_id}: no tasks resolved"
271	    done
272	  done
273	}

275	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
276	# preflight: rebuild harbor-runner if image-content-sha drifted
277	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
278	
279	# compute_harbor_source_sha mirrors build-harbor-runner.sh's algorithm: a
280	# sha256 over harbor_adapters/* + harbor_agent.py + Dockerfile.harbor-runner.
281	# Kept in sync by re-computing the same inputs; the two scripts MUST agree.
282	compute_harbor_source_sha() {
283	  local adapters_dir="${SCRIPT_DIR}/harbor_adapters"
284	  local agent_file="${SCRIPT_DIR}/harbor_agent.py"
285	  local dockerfile="${SCRIPT_DIR}/Dockerfile.harbor-runner"
286	  [[ -d "${adapters_dir}" ]] || die "preflight: harbor_adapters/ missing (${adapters_dir})"
287	  [[ -f "${agent_file}" ]] || die "preflight: harbor_agent.py missing (${agent_file})"
288	  [[ -f "${dockerfile}" ]] || die "preflight: Dockerfile.harbor-runner missing (${dockerfile})"
289	  {
290	    LC_ALL=C find "${adapters_dir}" -type f \
291	      | LC_ALL=C sort \
292	      | while IFS= read -r f; do
293	          printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${f}" | awk '{print $1}')" "${f#"${SCRIPT_DIR}/"}"
294	        done
295	    printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${agent_file}" | awk '{print $1}')" "harbor_agent.py"
296	    printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${dockerfile}" | awk '{print $1}')" "Dockerfile.harbor-runner"
297	  } | sha256sum | awk '{print $1}'
298	}
299	
300	# cached_harbor_image_sha prints the image-content-sha label of the cached
301	# image, or empty if the image is absent / the label is missing.
302	cached_harbor_image_sha() {
303	  local tag="$1"
304	  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || { printf ''; return 0; }
305	  docker image inspect --format '{{ index .Config.Labels "image-content-sha" }}' "${tag}" 2>/dev/null || printf ''
306	}
307	
308	cmd_preflight() {
309	  require_tool sha256sum
310	  local source_sha cached_sha
311	  source_sha="$(compute_harbor_source_sha)"
312	  cached_sha="$(cached_harbor_image_sha "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}")"
313	  printf 'preflight: source image-content-sha=%s\n' "${source_sha}"
314	  printf 'preflight: cached image-content-sha=%s\n' "${cached_sha:-<none>}"
315	  if [[ -n "${cached_sha}" && "${cached_sha}" == "${source_sha}" ]]; then
316	    printf 'preflight: harbor-runner image up to date; skipping rebuild\n'
317	    return 0
318	  fi
319	  [[ -x "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}" ]] || die "preflight: build script missing or not executable: ${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}"
320	  require_tool docker
321	  printf 'preflight: rebuilding harbor-runner (source sha differs)\n'
322	  IMAGE_TAG="${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}" "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}"
323	}

329	# Terminal final_status values that satisfy resume coverage. Non-terminal
330	# statuses (running, transient_exhausted, missing_result) do not count as
331	# coverage; --retry-invalid surfaces invalid_class != "" or orphan state.
332	is_terminal_status() {

348	# Mint a fresh cell-id: UTC timestamp + 4 random hex chars.
349	new_cell_id() {
350	  local ts rand
351	  ts="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
352	  rand="$(LC_ALL=C tr -dc 'a-f0-9' </dev/urandom 2>/dev/null | head -c 4 || true)"
353	  if [[ -z "${rand}" || ${#rand} -lt 4 ]]; then
354	    rand="$(printf '%04x' $((RANDOM & 0xFFFF)))"
355	  fi
356	  printf '%s-%s\n' "${ts}" "${rand}"
357	}

359	# task_executor_version: sha256 of the harbor task-executor script. The
360	# task-executor is shell, so the script bytes are the version of record.
361	task_executor_version() {
362	  local f="${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR}/harbor"
363	  if [[ -f "${f}" ]]; then
364	    sha256sum "${f}" | awk '{print $1}'
365	  else
366	    printf 'unknown\n'
367	  fi

370	# harbor_runner_image_digest: docker image Id for fizeau-harbor-runner. When
371	# docker is absent (CI / dry-run), fall back to the source-content sha so the
372	# sweep manifest is still well-defined.
373	harbor_runner_image_digest() {
374	  # Test/operator override wins so suites can pin the recorded digest.
375	  if [[ -n "${BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE:-}" ]]; then
376	    printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE}"
377	    return 0
378	  fi
379	  local tag="${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
380	  if command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
381	    local d
382	    d="$(docker image inspect --format '{{.Id}}' "${tag}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
383	    if [[ -n "${d}" ]]; then
384	      printf '%s\n' "${d}"
385	      return 0
386	    fi
387	  fi
388	  # No docker (or image absent). Derive from the source-content sha used by
389	  # preflight.
390	  if compute_harbor_source_sha >/dev/null 2>&1; then
391	    printf 'src:%s\n' "$(compute_harbor_source_sha)"
392	  else
393	    printf 'unknown\n'
394	  fi
395	}

413	# compose_env_redacted prints command-spec env with secret_env_keys replaced
414	# by a redaction sentinel. Order-insensitive; entries missing from env are
415	# ignored.
416	compose_env_redacted() {
417	  local env_json="$1" secret_keys_json="$2"
418	  jq -c --argjson secrets "${secret_keys_json}" '
419	    . as $env
420	    | reduce ($secrets[]? | tostring) as $k
421	        ($env; if has($k) then .[$k] = "***REDACTED***" else . end)
422	  ' <<<"${env_json}"
423	}

425	# is_transient_stderr greps the executor's stderr for known transient error
426	# signatures: connection refused/reset, HTTP 5xx, unexpected/EOF parse.
427	is_transient_stderr() {
428	  local f="$1"
429	  [[ -s "${f}" ]] || return 1
430	  LC_ALL=C grep -qE 'connection refused|connection reset|HTTP 5[0-9][0-9]|status[ =:]?5[0-9][0-9]|status_code=5[0-9][0-9]|unexpected EOF|EOF parse|EOF while|temporarily unavailable' "${f}"
431	}
432	
433	# Invoke the task-executor with bounded exponential backoff on transient
434	# errors. Returns 0 on success; non-zero on terminal failure after retries.
435	# Captures attempts log to ${cell_dir}/retry-log.txt for forensics.
436	run_executor_with_retry() {
437	  local task_spec="$1" cell_dir="$2" executor="$3"
438	  local stderr_file
439	  stderr_file="$(mktemp)"
440	  local max_attempts="${BENCH_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS}"
441	  local backoff="${BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE}"
442	  local attempt=1
443	  local status=0
444	  local retry_log="${cell_dir}/retry-log.txt"
445	  : >"${retry_log}"
446	  while :; do
447	    : >"${stderr_file}"
448	    set +e
449	    printf '%s' "${task_spec}" | "${executor}" 2>"${stderr_file}" >/dev/null
450	    status=$?
451	    set -e
452	    printf 'attempt=%d status=%d\n' "${attempt}" "${status}" >>"${retry_log}"
453	    if [[ ${status} -eq 0 ]]; then
454	      rm -f "${stderr_file}"
455	      return 0
456	    fi
457	    cat "${stderr_file}" >>"${retry_log}"
458	    if (( attempt > max_attempts )) || ! is_transient_stderr "${stderr_file}"; then
459	      cat "${stderr_file}" >&2
460	      rm -f "${stderr_file}"
461	      return "${status}"
462	    fi
463	    warn "cell $(basename "${cell_dir}"): transient error (attempt ${attempt}); sleeping ${backoff}s"
464	    sleep "${backoff}"
465	    backoff=$((backoff * 2))
466	    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
467	  done
468	}

470	# resolve_tasks_dir derives the host tasks dir for terminal-bench cells.
471	# Honors BENCH_TASKS_DIR override (used by tests).
472	resolve_tasks_dir() {
473	  local framework="$1"
474	  if [[ -n "${BENCH_TASKS_DIR:-}" ]]; then
475	    printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_TASKS_DIR}"
476	    return 0
477	  fi
478	  case "${framework}" in
479	    terminal-bench) printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_TASKS_DIR_DEFAULT}" ;;
480	    *)              printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_TASKS_DIR_DEFAULT}" ;;
481	  esac

484	# Count existing terminal cells matching (profile_id, task) under <out>; also
485	# emit lists of invalid cells and orphan cells (cells with cell-state.json
486	# and no terminal report.json). Lines are written to the named output paths.
487	inspect_existing_cells() {
488	  local out="$1" dataset_dir="$2" task="$3" profile_id="$4"
489	  local terminal_out="$5" invalid_out="$6" orphan_out="$7"
490	  : >"${terminal_out}"
491	  : >"${invalid_out}"
492	  : >"${orphan_out}"
493	  local task_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}"
494	  [[ -d "${task_dir}" ]] || return 0
495	  local cell
496	  shopt -s nullglob
497	  for cell in "${task_dir}"/*/; do
498	    local cdir="${cell%/}"
499	    local report="${cdir}/report.json"
500	    local state="${cdir}/cell-state.json"
501	    if [[ -f "${report}" ]]; then
502	      local pid status inv
503	      pid="$(jq -r '.profile.id // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
504	      [[ "${pid}" == "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
505	      status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
506	      inv="$(jq -r '.invalid_class // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
507	      if [[ -n "${inv}" ]]; then
508	        printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${invalid_out}"
509	        continue
510	      fi
511	      if is_terminal_status "${status}"; then
512	        printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${terminal_out}"
513	      fi
514	      continue
515	    fi
516	    if [[ -f "${state}" ]]; then
517	      local pid
518	      pid="$(jq -r '.profile // ""' "${state}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
519	      [[ -z "${pid}" || "${pid}" == "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
520	      printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${orphan_out}"
521	    fi
522	  done
523	  shopt -u nullglob
524	}

526	# Run one cell start-to-finish: cell-state sentinel, harness-adapter command,
527	# task-spec composition, task-executor invocation with retry, report.json
528	# composition, sentinel cleanup, optional back-write of superseded_by into
529	# the prior (attempt_of) cell.
530	run_one_cell() {
531	  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
532	  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
533	  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" harness_adapter="${10}"
534	  local attempt_of="${11}"
535	  local te_ver="${12}" hd="${13}"
536	
537	  local cell_id cell_dir
538	  cell_id="$(new_cell_id)"
539	  cell_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}/${cell_id}"
540	  mkdir -p "${cell_dir}"
541	
542	  local started_at
543	  started_at="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
544	  jq -n \
545	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
546	    --arg task "${task}" \
547	    --arg profile "${profile_id}" \
548	    --arg started "${started_at}" \
549	    '{cell_id:$cell_id, task:$task, profile:$profile, started_at:$started, status:"running"}' \
550	    >"${cell_dir}/cell-state.json"
551	
552	  local profile_json
553	  profile_json="$(yq -o=json '.' "${profile_file}")"
554	
555	  local adapter_path="${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR}/${harness_adapter}"
556	  [[ -x "${adapter_path}" ]] || die "harness-adapter missing/not executable: ${adapter_path}"
557	
558	  local command_spec install_spec harbor_plugin
559	  install_spec="$("${adapter_path}" install "/installed-agent/${harness_adapter}")" \
560	    || die "harness-adapter ${harness_adapter} install failed"
561	  harbor_plugin="$(jq -r '.harbor_plugin // ""' <<<"${install_spec}")"
562	  command_spec="$(printf '%s' "${profile_json}" | "${adapter_path}" command)" \
563	    || die "harness-adapter ${harness_adapter} command failed"
564	
565	  local env_json secret_keys_json command_json env_redacted
566	  env_json="$(jq -c '.env // {}' <<<"${command_spec}")"
567	  secret_keys_json="$(jq -c '.secret_env_keys // []' <<<"${command_spec}")"
568	  command_json="$(jq -c '.command // []' <<<"${command_spec}")"
569	  env_redacted="$(compose_env_redacted "${env_json}" "${secret_keys_json}")"
570	
571	  local tasks_dir
572	  tasks_dir="$(resolve_tasks_dir "${framework}")"
573	
574	  local task_spec
575	  task_spec="$(jq -nc \
576	    --arg task_id "${task}" \
577	    --arg tasks_dir "${tasks_dir}" \
578	    --arg cell_dir "${cell_dir}" \
579	    --arg plugin "${harbor_plugin}" \
580	    --arg image "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}" \
581	    --argjson env "${env_json}" \
582	    --argjson secret_env_keys "${secret_keys_json}" \
583	    '{task_id:$task_id, tasks_dir:$tasks_dir, cell_dir:$cell_dir,
584	      harbor_plugin:$plugin, image:$image, env:$env,
585	      secret_env_keys:$secret_env_keys, extra_args:[]}')"
586	
587	  local executor="${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR}/harbor"
588	  if [[ -n "${BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE:-}" ]]; then
589	    executor="${BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE}"
590	  fi
591	  [[ -x "${executor}" ]] || die "task-executor missing/not executable: ${executor}"
592	
593	  local exec_status=0
594	  run_executor_with_retry "${task_spec}" "${cell_dir}" "${executor}" || exec_status=$?
595	
596	  local finished_at
597	  finished_at="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
598	
599	  local result_json="{}"
600	  if [[ -s "${cell_dir}/result.json" ]]; then
601	    if jq -e '.' "${cell_dir}/result.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
602	      result_json="$(cat "${cell_dir}/result.json")"
603	    fi
604	  fi
605	
606	  local final_status invalid_class
607	  if [[ ${exec_status} -eq 0 ]]; then
608	    final_status="$(jq -r '.final_status // .status // "completed"' <<<"${result_json}")"
609	    invalid_class="$(jq -r '.invalid_class // ""' <<<"${result_json}")"
610	  else
611	    final_status="transient_exhausted"
612	    invalid_class="$(jq -r '.invalid_class // "transient_exhausted"' <<<"${result_json}")"
613	  fi
614	
615	  [[ -f "${cell_dir}/fiz.txt" ]] || : >"${cell_dir}/fiz.txt"
616	  [[ -f "${cell_dir}/fiz.err" ]] || : >"${cell_dir}/fiz.err"
617	  mkdir -p "${cell_dir}/session"
618	
619	  local rep_str
620	  rep_str="$(printf '%d/%d' "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}")"
621	
622	  local report
623	  report="$(jq -n \
624	    --argjson profile "${profile_json}" \
625	    --argjson command "${command_json}" \
626	    --argjson env_redacted "${env_redacted}" \
627	    --argjson result "${result_json}" \
628	    --arg task_id "${task}" \
629	    --arg framework "${framework}" \
630	    --arg dataset "${dataset}" \
631	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
632	    --arg started_at "${started_at}" \
633	    --arg finished_at "${finished_at}" \
634	    --arg final_status "${final_status}" \
635	    --arg invalid_class "${invalid_class}" \
636	    --arg te_ver "${te_ver}" \
637	    --arg hd "${hd}" \
638	    --arg rep "${rep_str}" \
639	    --arg attempt_of "${attempt_of}" \
640	    '{
641	       task_id:$task_id,
642	       framework:$framework,
643	       dataset:$dataset,
644	       cell_id:$cell_id,
645	       started_at:$started_at,
646	       finished_at:$finished_at,
647	       rep:$rep,
648	       profile:$profile,
649	       command:$command,
650	       env_redacted:$env_redacted,
651	       artifacts:{fiz_txt:"fiz.txt", fiz_err:"fiz.err", session_dir:"session"},
652	       result:$result,
653	       final_status:$final_status,
654	       invalid_class:$invalid_class,
655	       task_executor_version:$te_ver,
656	       harbor_runner_image_digest:$hd
657	     } | if $attempt_of != "" then . + {attempt_of:$attempt_of} else . end')"
658	
659	  printf '%s\n' "${report}" >"${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp"
660	  mv "${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp" "${cell_dir}/report.json"
661	
662	  rm -f "${cell_dir}/cell-state.json"
663	
664	  if [[ -n "${attempt_of}" && -f "${attempt_of}/report.json" ]]; then
665	    local tmp
666	    tmp="$(mktemp)"
667	    jq --arg sup "${cell_id}" '. + {superseded_by:$sup}' \
668	      "${attempt_of}/report.json" >"${tmp}"
669	    mv "${tmp}" "${attempt_of}/report.json"
670	  elif [[ -n "${attempt_of}" ]]; then
671	    # Orphan prior cell with no report.json: write a minimal stub so the
672	    # superseded_by back-link is recorded.
673	    jq -n \
674	      --arg sup "${cell_id}" \
675	      --arg orphan_of "$(basename "${attempt_of}")" \
676	      '{final_status:"orphan", invalid_class:"orphan_cell_state", cell_id:$orphan_of, superseded_by:$sup}' \
677	      >"${attempt_of}/report.json"
678	    rm -f "${attempt_of}/cell-state.json"
679	  fi
680	
681	  printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
682	}

684	# run_sweep walks the (profile, bench-set, task, rep) matrix, applies resume /
685	# retry rules, and runs one cell per slot.
686	run_sweep() {
687	  local profiles_csv="$1" bench_sets_csv="$2" out="$3"
688	  local force_rerun="$4" retry_invalid="$5" reps_override="$6"
689	
690	  require_tool yq
691	  require_tool jq
692	  require_tool sha256sum
693	
694	  mkdir -p "${out}"
695	
696	  local te_ver hd
697	  te_ver="$(task_executor_version)"
698	  hd="$(harbor_runner_image_digest)"
699	  write_sweep_metadata "${out}" "${te_ver}" "${hd}"
700	
701	  IFS=',' read -r -a profile_ids <<<"${profiles_csv}"
702	  IFS=',' read -r -a bench_set_ids <<<"${bench_sets_csv}"
703	
704	  local profile_id bs_id bs_file profile_file
705	  local framework dataset dataset_dir reps tasks harness adapter
706	  local terminal_file invalid_file orphan_file
707	
708	  for profile_id in "${profile_ids[@]}"; do
709	    [[ -n "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
710	    profile_file="$(resolve_profile_file "${profile_id}")"
711	    local profile_json_full harness
712	    profile_json_full="$(yq -o=json '.' "${profile_file}")"
713	    harness="$(jq -r '.harness // "none"' <<<"${profile_json_full}")"
714	    adapter="$(resolve_adapter_for_harness "${harness}")"
715	
716	    for bs_id in "${bench_set_ids[@]}"; do
717	      [[ -n "${bs_id}" ]] || continue
718	      bs_file="$(resolve_bench_set_file "${bs_id}")"
719	      framework="$(bench_set_framework "${bs_file}")"
720	      dataset="$(bench_set_dataset "${bs_file}")"
721	      dataset_dir="${dataset}"
722	      if [[ -n "${reps_override}" ]]; then
723	        reps="${reps_override}"
724	      else
725	        reps="$(bench_set_reps "${bs_file}")"
726	      fi
727	      tasks="$(resolve_tasks "${bs_file}")"
728	
729	      local task
730	      while IFS= read -r task; do
731	        [[ -n "${task}" ]] || continue
732	        [[ "${task}" != "*all*" ]] || die "bench-set ${bs_id}: tasks: all is not supported by run path yet"
733	
734	        terminal_file="$(mktemp)"; invalid_file="$(mktemp)"; orphan_file="$(mktemp)"
735	        inspect_existing_cells "${out}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" "${profile_id}" \
736	          "${terminal_file}" "${invalid_file}" "${orphan_file}"
737	
738	        local terminal_count invalid_count orphan_count
739	        terminal_count="$(wc -l <"${terminal_file}" | tr -d ' ')"
740	        invalid_count="$(wc -l <"${invalid_file}" | tr -d ' ')"
741	        orphan_count="$(wc -l <"${orphan_file}" | tr -d ' ')"
742	
743	        if [[ ${force_rerun} -eq 1 ]]; then
744	          local r
745	          for ((r = 1; r <= reps; r++)); do
746	            run_one_cell "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
747	              "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
748	              "${r}" "${reps}" "${adapter}" "" "${te_ver}" "${hd}" >/dev/null
749	          done
750	        elif [[ ${retry_invalid} -eq 1 ]]; then
751	          local prior
752	          while IFS= read -r prior; do
753	            [[ -n "${prior}" ]] || continue
754	            run_one_cell "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
755	              "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
756	              "1" "${reps}" "${adapter}" "${prior}" "${te_ver}" "${hd}" >/dev/null
757	          done <"${invalid_file}"
758	          while IFS= read -r prior; do
759	            [[ -n "${prior}" ]] || continue
760	            run_one_cell "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
761	              "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
762	              "1" "${reps}" "${adapter}" "${prior}" "${te_ver}" "${hd}" >/dev/null
763	          done <"${orphan_file}"
764	          warn "retry-invalid: profile=${profile_id} task=${task} reran invalid=${invalid_count} orphan=${orphan_count}"
765	        else
766	          if [[ ${terminal_count} -ge ${reps} ]]; then
767	            warn "skip: profile=${profile_id} task=${task} (terminal_cells=${terminal_count} >= reps=${reps})"
768	          else
769	            local need r idx
770	            need=$((reps - terminal_count))
771	            idx=$((terminal_count + 1))
772	            for ((r = 1; r <= need; r++)); do
773	              run_one_cell "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
774	                "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
775	                "${idx}" "${reps}" "${adapter}" "" "${te_ver}" "${hd}" >/dev/null
776	              idx=$((idx + 1))
777	            done
778	          fi
779	        fi
780	
781	        rm -f "${terminal_file}" "${invalid_file}" "${orphan_file}"
782	      done <<<"${tasks}"
783	    done
784	  done
785	}
786	
787	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
788	# validate (placeholder until PR C)
789	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
790	
791	cmd_validate() {
792	  warn "validate: schema + cross-reference checks land in PR C; this is a placeholder"
793	  return 0
794	}
795	
796	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
797	# main dispatch
798	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
799	
800	main() {
801	  if [[ $# -eq 0 ]]; then
802	    usage
803	    exit 2
804	  fi
805	
806	  local profile_csv=""
807	  local bench_set_csv=""
808	  local plan=0
809	  local out=""
810	  local force_rerun=0
811	  local retry_invalid=0
812	  local reps_override=""
813	  local positional=()
814	
815	  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
816	    case "$1" in
817	      --profile)        profile_csv="${2:?--profile requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
818	      --bench-set)      bench_set_csv="${2:?--bench-set requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
819	      --plan)           plan=1; shift ;;
820	      --out)            out="${2:?--out requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
821	      --force-rerun)    force_rerun=1; shift ;;
822	      --retry-invalid)  retry_invalid=1; shift ;;
823	      --reps)           reps_override="${2:?--reps requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
824	      -h|--help|help)   usage; exit 0 ;;
825	      --)               shift; positional+=("$@"); break ;;
826	      -*)               die "unknown flag: $1" ;;
827	      *)                positional+=("$1"); shift ;;
828	    esac
829	  done
830	
831	  local sub=""
832	  if [[ ${#positional[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
833	    sub="${positional[0]}"
834	  fi
835	
836	  case "${sub}" in
837	    profiles)          cmd_profiles ;;
838	    bench-sets)        cmd_bench_sets ;;
839	    harness-adapters)  cmd_harness_adapters ;;
840	    task-executors)    cmd_task_executors ;;
841	    validate)          cmd_validate ;;
842	    preflight)         cmd_preflight ;;
843	    "")
844	      require_tool yq
845	      require_tool jq
846	      [[ -n "${profile_csv}" ]]   || die "--profile is required"
847	      [[ -n "${bench_set_csv}" ]] || die "--bench-set is required"
848	      if [[ ${plan} -eq 1 ]]; then
849	        print_plan "${profile_csv}" "${bench_set_csv}"
850	        exit 0
851	      fi
852	      if [[ -z "${out}" ]]; then
853	        out="${DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT}"
854	      fi
855	      if [[ ${force_rerun} -eq 1 && ${retry_invalid} -eq 1 ]]; then
856	        die "--force-rerun and --retry-invalid are mutually exclusive"
857	      fi
858	      run_sweep "${profile_csv}" "${bench_set_csv}" "${out}" \
859	        "${force_rerun}" "${retry_invalid}" "${reps_override}"
860	      ;;
861	    *)
862	      die "unknown subcommand: ${sub}"
863	      ;;
864	  esac
865	}
866	
867	main "$@"

# Concurrency groups for the benchmark runner
#
# Flat {id, max_concurrency} mapping. Profiles reference these groups by id
# via their `concurrency_group:` field.
#
# Spec: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification.md (PR 1b)

groups:
  vidar-ds4:
    max_concurrency: 1
    description: >-
      DwarfStar 4 (antirez/ds4) on vidar (http://192.168.2.106:1236/v1).
      Single graph worker — strictly serialized. Mutually exclusive with
      vidar-omlx on the same hardware.

  vidar-omlx:
    max_concurrency: 1
    description: >-
      oMLX server on vidar (http://vidar:1235/v1). Single-model server;
      serialize all cells.

  bragi-club-3090:
    max_concurrency: 1
    description: >-
      vLLM with autoround quantization on bragi RTX 3090
      (http://bragi:8020/v1). Single-cell serialization.

  sindri-lucebox-dflash:
    max_concurrency: 1
    description: >-
      lucebox-hub DFlash speculative decoder on sindri
      (http://sindri:8080/v1, alias luce-dflash). Single-cell serialization.

  grendel-rapid-mlx:
    max_concurrency: 1
    description: >-
      Rapid-MLX server on grendel (http://grendel:8000/v1). Single-cell
      serialization.

  sindri-club-3090:
    max_concurrency: 1
    description: >-
      vLLM with autoround quantization on sindri RTX 3090
      (http://sindri:8020/v1). Mutually exclusive with
      sindri-club-3090-llamacpp (same machine + port).

  sindri-club-3090-llamacpp:
    max_concurrency: 1
    description: >-
      llama.cpp's llama-server on sindri (http://sindri:8020/v1) hosting
      Unsloth Qwen3.6-27B-UD-Q3_K_XL GGUF. Mutually exclusive with
      sindri-club-3090.

  openrouter:
    max_concurrency: 10
    description: >-
      Managed OpenRouter lane (https://openrouter.ai/api/v1). RPM 500,
      TPM 200000. Per-run cap $5; per-phase cap $50; per-sweep cap $150.

  openrouter-qwen36-27b:
    max_concurrency: 10
    description: >-
      Managed OpenRouter Qwen3.6-27B lane. Separated from openrouter so
      full Qwen runs do not share a limiter with Claude/GPT harness
      comparison lanes. RPM 500, TPM 1000000.

  openai-gpt55:
    max_concurrency: 16
    description: >-
      Native OpenAI GPT-5.5 lane (https://api.openai.com/v1). RPM 500,
      TPM 500000. Per-run cap $8; per-phase cap $300.

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Integration test for the ./benchmark run path (bead fizeau-a2fd070b).
3	#
4	# Exercises all five ACs of the bead description:
5	#
6	#  1. ./benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary produces
7	#     cells with report.json embedding profile, command, env_redacted +
8	#     fiz.txt, fiz.err, session/ artifacts.
9	#  2. <out>/sweep.json captures task_executor_version +
10	#     harbor_runner_image_digest; each cell's report.json references them.
11	#  3. Re-running without --force-rerun skips cells with terminal report.json.
12	#  4. --retry-invalid reruns invalid cells; new cell links via attempt_of and
13	#     prior cell receives superseded_by back-link.
14	#  5. Transient 5xx triggers bounded exponential backoff + eventual success.
15	#
16	# Uses HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 (the harbor task-executor's documented
17	# dry-run mode) for ACs 1–4 so docker is not required. AC5 swaps in a stub
18	# executor (BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE) that emits 5xx for the first few
19	# attempts and then succeeds, to drive the retry loop deterministically.
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
24	BENCHMARK="${SCRIPT_DIR}/benchmark"
25	
26	TMP_ROOT=""
27	cleanup() {
28	  if [[ -n "${TMP_ROOT}" && -d "${TMP_ROOT}" ]]; then
29	    rm -rf "${TMP_ROOT}"
30	  fi
31	}
32	trap cleanup EXIT
33	
34	fail() {
35	  echo "FAIL: $*" >&2
36	  exit 1
37	}
38	
39	assert_eq() {
40	  local got="$1" want="$2" msg="${3:-values differ}"
41	  [[ "${got}" == "${want}" ]] || fail "${msg}: got=${got} want=${want}"
42	}
43	
44	assert_nonempty() {
45	  local v="$1" msg="${2:-value is empty}"
46	  [[ -n "${v}" ]] || fail "${msg}"
47	}
48	
49	require() {
50	  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1 || fail "required tool not found: $1"
51	}
52	
53	require jq
54	require yq
55	require sha256sum
56	
57	TMP_ROOT="$(mktemp -d)"
58	TASKS_DIR="${TMP_ROOT}/tasks"
59	OUT_DIR="${TMP_ROOT}/out"
60	STUB_DIR="${TMP_ROOT}/stubs"
61	mkdir -p "${TASKS_DIR}" "${OUT_DIR}" "${STUB_DIR}"
62	
63	PROFILE="sindri-lucebox"
64	BENCH_SET="tb-2-1-canary"
65	DATASET="terminal-bench-2-1"
66	TASKS=(cancel-async-tasks log-summary-date-ranges configure-git-webserver)
67	
68	echo "==> AC1+AC2: initial sweep produces cells + sweep.json"
69	
70	env HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 \
71	    BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
72	    BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-aaa" \
73	    "${BENCHMARK}" \
74	      --profile "${PROFILE}" \
75	      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
76	      --reps 1 \
77	      --out "${OUT_DIR}" \
78	    >/dev/null
79	
80	# sweep.json present with both fields
81	sweep_json="${OUT_DIR}/sweep.json"
82	[[ -f "${sweep_json}" ]] || fail "sweep.json missing at ${sweep_json}"
83	te_ver="$(jq -r '.task_executor_version // ""' "${sweep_json}")"

87	assert_eq "${hd}" "sha256:test-digest-aaa" "sweep.json: digest mismatch"

116	  redacted="$(jq -r '.env_redacted.FIZEAU_API_KEY // ""' "${report}")"

122	  [[ -f "${cell}/fiz.err" ]] || fail "task=${task}: fiz.err missing"
123	  [[ -d "${cell}/session" ]] || fail "task=${task}: session/ missing"
124	
125	  # cell-state.json was cleaned up on terminal close
126	  [[ ! -f "${cell}/cell-state.json" ]] \

128	
129	  # final_status is terminal
130	  status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' "${report}")"
131	  case "${status}" in
132	    completed|pass|fail|timeout) ;;
133	    *) fail "task=${task}: unexpected final_status=${status}" ;;
134	  esac
135	done
136	
137	echo "==> AC3: rerun (no flag) skips terminal cells"
138	
139	before_count="$(find "${OUT_DIR}/cells" -mindepth 3 -maxdepth 3 -type d | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
140	env HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 \
141	    BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
142	    BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-aaa" \
143	    "${BENCHMARK}" \
144	      --profile "${PROFILE}" \
145	      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
146	      --reps 1 \
147	      --out "${OUT_DIR}" \
148	    >/dev/null
149	after_count="$(find "${OUT_DIR}/cells" -mindepth 3 -maxdepth 3 -type d | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
150	assert_eq "${after_count}" "${before_count}" "resume default added cells (${before_count} -> ${after_count})"
151	
152	echo "==> AC4: --retry-invalid reruns invalid cells with attempt_of + superseded_by"
153	
154	# Mark the cancel-async-tasks cell as invalid by injecting invalid_class.
155	prior_cell="$(find "${OUT_DIR}/cells/${DATASET}/cancel-async-tasks" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d | head -n1)"
156	[[ -n "${prior_cell}" ]] || fail "no prior cell found for cancel-async-tasks"
157	prior_report="${prior_cell}/report.json"
158	tmp_report="${TMP_ROOT}/prior_report.json"
159	jq '.invalid_class = "setup_failure" | .final_status = "invalid"' "${prior_report}" >"${tmp_report}"
160	mv "${tmp_report}" "${prior_report}"
161	prior_cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id' "${prior_report}")"
162	
163	env HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 \
164	    BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
165	    BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-aaa" \
166	    "${BENCHMARK}" \
167	      --profile "${PROFILE}" \
168	      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
169	      --reps 1 \
170	      --retry-invalid \
171	      --out "${OUT_DIR}" \
172	    >/dev/null
173	
174	# A new cell should now exist whose attempt_of points at the prior cell.
175	new_cell=""
176	for c in "${OUT_DIR}/cells/${DATASET}/cancel-async-tasks"/*/; do
177	  cdir="${c%/}"
178	  [[ "${cdir}" == "${prior_cell}" ]] && continue
179	  if [[ -f "${cdir}/report.json" ]]; then

192	echo "    prior=${prior_cell_id} superseded_by=${new_cell_id}"

195	# new cell links to it via attempt_of.
196	orphan_dir="${OUT_DIR}/cells/${DATASET}/log-summary-date-ranges/19700101T000000Z-dead"
197	mkdir -p "${orphan_dir}"

200	  >"${orphan_dir}/cell-state.json"
201	
202	env HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 \
203	    BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
204	    BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-aaa" \
205	    "${BENCHMARK}" \
206	      --profile "${PROFILE}" \
207	      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
208	      --reps 1 \
209	      --retry-invalid \
210	      --out "${OUT_DIR}" \
211	    >/dev/null
212	
213	orphan_report="${orphan_dir}/report.json"
214	[[ -f "${orphan_report}" ]] || fail "orphan cell did not receive back-written report.json"
215	orphan_super="$(jq -r '.superseded_by // ""' "${orphan_report}")"
216	assert_nonempty "${orphan_super}" "orphan cell missing superseded_by"
217	
218	found_orphan_retry=0
219	for c in "${OUT_DIR}/cells/${DATASET}/log-summary-date-ranges"/*/; do
220	  cdir="${c%/}"

222	  if [[ -f "${cdir}/report.json" ]]; then
223	    ao="$(jq -r '.attempt_of // ""' "${cdir}/report.json")"
224	    if [[ "${ao}" == "${orphan_dir}" ]]; then

232	echo "==> AC5: transient 5xx triggers exponential backoff + eventual success"

234	STATE_FILE="${TMP_ROOT}/transient-state"
235	echo 0 >"${STATE_FILE}"
236	cat >"${STUB_DIR}/flaky" <<EOF
237	#!/usr/bin/env bash
238	# Stub task-executor. Fails the first two attempts with HTTP 503 (a transient
239	# signature recognized by run_executor_with_retry), then succeeds.
240	set -euo pipefail

242	cell_dir=""
243	spec="\$(cat)"
244	cell_dir="\$(jq -r '.cell_dir' <<<"\$spec")"
245	n=\$(cat "\${state_file}")
246	n=\$((n + 1))
247	echo "\${n}" >"\${state_file}"
248	if (( n < 3 )); then

250	  exit 1
251	fi

254	  '{task_id:\$t, final_status:"completed", status:"completed"}' \\
255	  >"\$cell_dir/result.json"
256	EOF
257	chmod +x "${STUB_DIR}/flaky"
258	
259	OUT2="${TMP_ROOT}/out2"
260	mkdir -p "${OUT2}"
261	env BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
262	    BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE="${STUB_DIR}/flaky" \
263	    BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE=0 \

265	    "${BENCHMARK}" \

267	      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
268	      --reps 1 \
269	      --out "${OUT2}" \
270	    >/dev/null
271	
272	# The state file counter is shared across all cells in the sweep, so only the
273	# first task processed (cancel-async-tasks, per bench-set yaml order) sees the
274	# 5xx -> 5xx -> 200 retry sequence; later cells succeed immediately. Target
275	# that first cell explicitly.
276	first_cell="$(find "${OUT2}/cells/${DATASET}/cancel-async-tasks" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d | head -n1)"
277	[[ -n "${first_cell}" ]] || fail "transient sweep: no cell created"

279	[[ -f "${retry_log}" ]] || fail "transient sweep: retry-log.txt missing"
280

283	grep -q 'status=0' "${retry_log}" || fail "transient sweep: no successful attempt"
284	
285	final_status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' "${first_cell}/report.json")"
286	case "${final_status}" in
287	  completed|pass) ;;
288	  *) fail "transient sweep: expected terminal-success final_status, got ${final_status}" ;;
289	esac

291	# Terminal failure after retry cap: stub that always fails.
292	cat >"${STUB_DIR}/always_5xx" <<'EOF'
293	#!/usr/bin/env bash
294	set -euo pipefail
295	cat >/dev/null

301	OUT3="${TMP_ROOT}/out3"
302	mkdir -p "${OUT3}"

304	env BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
305	    BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE="${STUB_DIR}/always_5xx" \

309	    "${BENCHMARK}" \
310	      --profile "${PROFILE}" \
311	      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
312	      --reps 1 \
313	      --out "${OUT3}" \
314	    >/dev/null 2>&1
315	rc=$?
316	set -e
317	# The runner does not exit non-zero on per-cell failure today (the cell is
318	# written with final_status=transient_exhausted). Both behaviors are
319	# acceptable; the AC is satisfied by the cell record.
320	: "${rc}"

322	cap_cell="$(find "${OUT3}/cells/${DATASET}" -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 2 -type d | head -n1)"

ID:       fizeau-11e9c095
Title:    bench-pr-A: bash runner + shell adapters + harbor-runner image + --preset benchmark fix
Type:     task
Status:   proposed
Priority: 2
Labels:   area:bench, kind:feature, governing:plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification, pr:A
Parent:   fizeau-163efede
Desc:     First and largest piece of the bench runner rewrite. Replaces cmd/bench/matrix.go with
a bash runner; ports the matrix-side Python harness_adapters/ to shell harness-adapters/;
wraps Harbor invocation in a Docker container so the host stops needing Python.

CONTEXT: today's runner is `fiz-bench matrix` (Go, cmd/bench/matrix.go ~2232 LOC). It
puts repo root on host PYTHONPATH (cmd/bench/matrix.go:1105) so Harbor can import
scripts.benchmark.harbor_adapters.*. That makes host Python required. We're moving
Harbor and its plugins into a fizeau-harbor-runner Docker image so the host only needs
bash + Node + Docker. The new runner is at scripts/benchmark/benchmark (no extension).

SCOPE — runner (scripts/benchmark/benchmark, new bash script):
  - Arg parsing + dispatch: ./benchmark <subcommand> ... with subcommands
      profiles | bench-sets | task-executors | harness-adapters | validate |
      preflight | (no subcommand = run mode)
  - Run mode flags: --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] [--plan]
      [--force-rerun] [--retry-invalid] [--reps N] [--jobs N] [--max-cost-usd X]
      [--out DIR]
  - Read profile/bench-set/concurrency-group YAML with `yq -o=json`.
  - Matrix expansion: profile × task × rep -> ordered cell list. Resolve `tasks_from:`
    references (path relative to bench-set file) AND inline `tasks:` lists. PR C will
    finalize the schema; for PR A's smoke tests, default `task_executor` to `harbor`
    when `framework == terminal-bench`.
  - Resume default: if cell_dir/report.json exists with terminal final_status -> skip.
    --force-rerun ignores. --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class OR
    orphan cell-state.json.
  - Per-cell loop: write cell-state.json sentinel; invoke
    harness-adapters/<name> command < profile.json -> command-spec.json; compose
    task-spec; invoke task-executors/<name> < task-spec.json (writes
    cell_dir/result.json); compose report.json; delete cell-state.json.
  - Concurrency: flock per concurrency-group at
    \${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-\${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-\$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock;
    setsid for each cell process group; background-job pool capped by --jobs.
  - Signal handling: trap SIGINT/SIGTERM. Stop accepting new cells. SIGTERM each
    in-flight cell's process group and `docker stop` each in-flight harbor-runner
    container. Wait 30s, then SIGKILL. Write final_status=interrupted to in-flight
    cells (process_outcome=killed, no metrics). Exit non-zero.
  - Concurrent-cells state file at
    \${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}/<hostname>/in-flight.json. Runner appends its cell at
    start under flock, reads current count, writes into cell, removes at close.
    Prune stale entries (PID dead) at next read.
  - Budget: compute cost_usd_at_run_time per cell from profile.pricing × token streams
    at close. Accumulate into <out>/budget.json. Halt before next cell when cap hit.
    Skipped cells get final_status=budget_halted placeholder with process_outcome=
    setup_failed and a 'note' field.
  - Per-cell retry: bounded exponential backoff for transient errors (connection
    refused, 5xx, EOF parse). Write attempt_of: link in the new cell; back-write
    superseded_by: into the prior cell (the one allowed mutation of a closed cell).

SCOPE — harbor-runner Docker image:
  - scripts/benchmark/Dockerfile.harbor-runner:
      FROM python:3.12-slim
      RUN pip install --no-cache-dir harbor-bench==<pinned-version>
      COPY scripts/benchmark/harbor_adapters/ /app/harbor_adapters/
      COPY scripts/benchmark/harbor_agent.py /app/harbor_agent.py
      ENV PYTHONPATH=/app
      WORKDIR /app
      ENTRYPOINT [\"harbor\"]
  - scripts/benchmark/build-harbor-runner.sh: docker build with image-content-sha
    label computed from sha256 of harbor_adapters/ + harbor_agent.py +
    Dockerfile.harbor-runner. ./benchmark preflight triggers rebuild if cached image
    label doesn't match current sha.
  - Pre-PR-A spike (5 min): confirm `pip install harbor-bench==<pinned>` works. If
    Harbor is git-only, use `pip install git+https://github.com/laude-institute/...`.

SCOPE — shell adapters (8 harness-adapters + 1 task-executor):
  - scripts/benchmark/task-executors/harbor: bash wrapper around
      docker run --rm -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \\
        -v \"\$cell_dir:/output\" -v \"\$tasks_dir:/tasks:ro\" \\
        -e FIZEAU_API_KEY -e FIZEAU_BASE_URL -e FIZEAU_MODEL ... \\
        fizeau-harbor-runner:latest run \\
        --agent-import-path \"\$harbor_plugin\" --task-id \"\$task_id\" --output /output ...
    Reads task-spec.json from stdin (see CONTRACT.md). Writes cell_dir/result.json.
    Captures task_executor_version + harbor_runner_image_digest once per ./benchmark
    invocation into <out>/sweep.json; runner copies into each cell.
  - scripts/benchmark/harness-adapters/{fiz, claude, codex, opencode, pi, cost-probe,
    noop, dumb-script}: bash ports of scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/*.py. Each
    is executable with two subcommands and a '# SUMMARY:' header line.
      <name> install <agent-artifact-path> -> install-spec.json on stdout with
        {install_command, artifact_source, binary_path, harbor_plugin} where
        harbor_plugin names the Python class Harbor loads (e.g.
        'scripts.benchmark.harbor_adapters.fiz:FizeauAgent').
      <name> command (reads profile JSON on stdin) -> command-spec.json on stdout

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# SUMMARY: harbor task-executor. Reads task-spec.json on stdin; runs `docker run --rm fizeau-harbor-runner:latest run --agent-import-path … --task-id … --output /output …`; writes cell_dir/result.json. The docker socket is mounted so harbor can spawn the task container.
3	set -euo pipefail
4	
5	usage() {
6	  cat >&2 <<'EOF'
7	Usage:
8	  harbor  (reads task-spec.json on stdin)
9	
10	task-spec.json fields (see scripts/benchmark/task-executors/CONTRACT.md):
11	  task_id         string  TerminalBench task id
12	  tasks_dir       string  host dir holding task definitions; bind-mounted at /tasks (ro)
13	  cell_dir        string  host dir for cell artifacts; bind-mounted at /output (rw)
14	  harbor_plugin   string  e.g. "scripts.benchmark.harbor_agent:FizeauAgent"
15	  image           string  default "fizeau-harbor-runner:latest"
16	  env             object  env to forward into the container
17	  secret_env_keys [str]   subset of env keys whose values are secrets (redacted in logs)
18	  extra_args      [str]   passthrough trailing args appended after --output /output
19	EOF
20	  return 2
21	}
22	
23	if [[ "${1:-}" == "-h" || "${1:-}" == "--help" ]]; then
24	  usage
25	fi
26	
27	spec="$(cat)"
28	if [[ -z "$spec" ]]; then
29	  echo "harbor: empty task-spec on stdin" >&2
30	  exit 2
31	fi
32	
33	task_id="$(jq -r '.task_id // ""' <<<"$spec")"
34	tasks_dir="$(jq -r '.tasks_dir // ""' <<<"$spec")"
35	cell_dir="$(jq -r '.cell_dir // ""' <<<"$spec")"
36	plugin="$(jq -r '.harbor_plugin // ""' <<<"$spec")"
37	image="$(jq -r '.image // "fizeau-harbor-runner:latest"' <<<"$spec")"
38	
39	if [[ -z "$task_id" || -z "$tasks_dir" || -z "$cell_dir" || -z "$plugin" ]]; then
40	  echo "harbor: task-spec missing one of {task_id, tasks_dir, cell_dir, harbor_plugin}" >&2
41	  exit 2
42	fi
43	if [[ ! -d "$tasks_dir" ]]; then
44	  echo "harbor: tasks_dir does not exist: $tasks_dir" >&2
45	  exit 2
46	fi
47	mkdir -p "$cell_dir"
48	
49	declare -a docker_args=(
50	  run --rm
51	  -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
52	  -v "$cell_dir:/output"
53	  -v "$tasks_dir:/tasks:ro"
54	)
55	
56	while IFS=$'\t' read -r key val; do
57	  [[ -z "$key" ]] && continue
58	  docker_args+=(-e "$key=$val")
59	done < <(jq -r '(.env // {}) | to_entries[] | "\(.key)\t\(.value)"' <<<"$spec")
60	
61	docker_args+=("$image" run
62	  --agent-import-path "$plugin"

64	  --tasks-dir /tasks
65	  --output /output
66	)
67	
68	while IFS= read -r arg; do

72	
73	result_path="$cell_dir/result.json"
74

76	  jq -n \
77	    --arg image "$image" \
78	    --arg task "$task_id" \
79	    --arg plugin "$plugin" \
80	    --argjson argv "$(printf '%s\n' "${docker_args[@]}" | jq -R . | jq -s .)" \
81	    '{dry_run:true, image:$image, task_id:$task, harbor_plugin:$plugin, docker_argv:$argv}' \
82	    >"$result_path"

# task-executors contract

Each file under `scripts/benchmark/task-executors/` is an executable shell
script that runs one task for one cell. The benchmark runner streams a
**task-spec.json** to the executor on stdin; the executor writes
**result.json** into the cell directory and exits.

Today there is one executor:

- `harbor` — runs the task inside the `fizeau-harbor-runner` Docker image.
  Harbor handles task setup, agent invocation, grading, and trajectory
  capture; the executor is a thin shell wrapper that mounts the cell dir
  and forwards env.

## Header convention

Every executor file starts with a single `# SUMMARY:` line on line 2
(after the shebang). The benchmark runner's
`./benchmark task-executors` listing greps for this header.

```
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# SUMMARY: <one-line description>
```

## task-spec.json (stdin)

```json
{
  "task_id":         "patch-build-script",
  "tasks_dir":       "/home/erik/Projects/fizeau/external/terminal-bench/tasks",
  "cell_dir":        "/home/erik/.../cells/terminal-bench-2-1/patch-build-script/20260516T103045Z-a4c1",
  "harbor_plugin":   "scripts.benchmark.harbor_agent:FizeauAgent",
  "image":           "fizeau-harbor-runner:latest",
  "env": {
    "FIZEAU_BASE_URL": "http://vidar:1234/v1",
    "FIZEAU_MODEL":    "qwen3.6-27b",
    "FIZEAU_API_KEY":  "${FIZEAU_API_KEY}"
  },
  "secret_env_keys": ["FIZEAU_API_KEY"],
  "extra_args":      ["--jobs-dir", "/jobs", "--job-name", "20260516T103045Z-a4c1"]
}
```

Fields:

- `task_id` (required) — TerminalBench task id; the executor passes this
  to `harbor run --task-id`.
- `tasks_dir` (required, host path) — directory containing the task
  definitions. Bind-mounted at `/tasks` read-only.
- `cell_dir` (required, host path) — directory the runner already created
  for this cell. Bind-mounted at `/output` read/write. Harbor writes
  `result.json`, logs, and trajectory artifacts here.
- `harbor_plugin` (required) — Python import path passed to
  `harbor run --agent-import-path`. Sourced from
  `harness-adapters/<name> install` output.
- `image` (optional, default `fizeau-harbor-runner:latest`) — container
  image tag. The runner overrides this when running a sha-pinned image.
- `env` (optional) — env vars forwarded with `-e KEY=VALUE` into the
  container. Values containing `${VAR}` are expanded against the
  runner's environment before docker invocation. Sourced from
  `harness-adapters/<name> command` output.
- `secret_env_keys` (optional) — subset of `env` keys flagged as
  secrets; values are redacted in any record the runner persists.
- `extra_args` (optional) — passthrough trailing args appended to
  `harbor run … --output /output`. Used for `--jobs-dir`, `--job-name`,
  `--reps`, harbor-side timeout multipliers, etc.

## Behavior

1. The runner has already created `cell_dir` and may have written a
   `cell-state.json` sentinel.
2. The executor reads stdin, validates the spec, and invokes:

   ```
   docker run --rm \
     -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \
     -v "$cell_dir:/output" \
     -v "$tasks_dir:/tasks:ro" \
     -e KEY1=VAL1 -e KEY2=VAL2 ... \
     <image> run \
       --agent-import-path <plugin> \
       --task-id <task_id> \
       --tasks-dir /tasks \
       --output /output \
       <extra_args...>
   ```

3. Harbor writes `result.json` (and supporting artifacts) to
   `/output`, which is `cell_dir` on the host.
4. The executor exits with docker's exit code. If Harbor crashed without
   producing `result.json`, the executor writes a stub
   `{task_id, image, harbor_plugin, exit_code, status:"missing_result"}`
   so the runner can classify the cell.

## Dry-run mode

Setting `HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1` skips `docker run` entirely;
the executor writes the would-be docker argv to `cell_dir/result.json`
as `{dry_run:true, image, task_id, harbor_plugin, docker_argv}` and
echoes the same JSON to stdout. Used by `./benchmark --plan` and
shellcheck-driven smokes.

## Validation

`./benchmark validate` calls every executor with a synthetic task-spec
under `HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1` and rejects:

- non-zero exit;
- a `cell_dir/result.json` that is not valid JSON;
- missing required spec fields surfaced as `exit 2`.

`shellcheck` (CI gate) must pass on every file in this directory.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Cell dispatch under flock + setsid; signal handling
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# write_interrupted_report stamps a minimal interrupted record on a cell when
# the runner is signalled mid-flight. Preserves any partial result.json on
# disk via the .result passthrough so debugging can use it. Overrides
# final_status to "interrupted" with process_outcome=killed and no metrics.
write_interrupted_report() {
  local cell_dir="$1" cell_id="$2" profile_id="$3"
  [[ -n "${cell_dir}" && -d "${cell_dir}" ]] || return 0
  local report="${cell_dir}/report.json"
  local finished_at
  finished_at="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"

  local existing='{}'
  if [[ -s "${report}" ]] && jq -e . "${report}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    existing="$(cat "${report}")"
    # Honor reports the cell itself wrote out before being signalled if they
    # already declare a terminal status; the bead contract only covers cells
    # still in-flight at SIGTERM time.
    local cur_status
    cur_status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' <<<"${existing}")"
    if is_terminal_status "${cur_status}"; then
      return 0
    fi
  fi

  local result_partial='{}'
  if [[ -s "${cell_dir}/result.json" ]] && jq -e . "${cell_dir}/result.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    result_partial="$(cat "${cell_dir}/result.json")"
  fi

  local tmp; tmp="$(mktemp)"
  jq -n \
    --argjson existing "${existing}" \
    --argjson partial "${result_partial}" \
    --arg cid "${cell_id}" \
    --arg profile "${profile_id}" \
    --arg fin "${finished_at}" \
    '($existing
      | (.cell_id // $cid) as $kept_cid
      | . + {
          cell_id: $kept_cid,
          final_status: "interrupted",
          process_outcome: "killed",
          finished_at: $fin,
          invalid_class: (.invalid_class // ""),
          result: $partial
        })' >"${tmp}"
  mv "${tmp}" "${report}"
  rm -f "${cell_dir}/cell-state.json"
}

# __cell_runner_main: re-entry point used when the parent runner forks a cell
# under setsid. Reads a JSON spec on stdin, acquires the concurrency-group
# flock, registers the cell in in-flight.json, runs the cell, and removes
# itself from in-flight on exit.
__cell_runner_main() {
  require_tool jq
  require_tool yq
  require_tool flock

  local spec
  spec="$(cat)"
  [[ -n "${spec}" ]] || { echo "__cell-runner: empty spec on stdin" >&2; exit 2; }

  local lock_file cg cell_id cell_dir container_name profile_id
  lock_file="$(jq -r '.lock_file' <<<"${spec}")"
  cg="$(jq -r '.concurrency_group' <<<"${spec}")"
  cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id' <<<"${spec}")"
  cell_dir="$(jq -r '.cell_dir' <<<"${spec}")"
  container_name="$(jq -r '.container_name' <<<"${spec}")"
  profile_id="$(jq -r '.profile_id' <<<"${spec}")"

  mkdir -p "$(dirname "${lock_file}")"
  exec 200>"${lock_file}"
  flock 200

  local entry
  entry="$(jq -n \
    --arg cid "${cell_id}" \
    --arg cdir "${cell_dir}" \
    --arg cn "${container_name}" \
    --arg p "${profile_id}" \
    --arg cg "${cg}" \
    --arg ts "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
    --argjson pid "$$" \
    '{cell_id:$cid, cell_dir:$cdir, container_name:$cn,
      pid:$pid, pgid:$pid,
      profile:$p, concurrency_group:$cg, started_at:$ts}')"
  inflight_add "${entry}"

  # shellcheck disable=SC2064  # expansion on trap install is intentional
  trap "inflight_remove '${cell_id}' >/dev/null 2>&1 || true" EXIT

  export BENCH_CELL_CONTAINER_NAME="${container_name}"

  local run_args_json
  run_args_json="$(jq -c '.run_args' <<<"${spec}")"
  local -a run_args=()
  while IFS= read -r v; do
    run_args+=("${v}")
  done < <(jq -r '.[]' <<<"${run_args_json}")

  run_one_cell "${run_args[@]}" >/dev/null
}

# launch_cell forks a setsid'd child running __cell-runner. The child holds
# the per-concurrency-group flock for its full lifetime; the parent tracks
# the child's PID/PGID so signal-handling can SIGTERM the whole group and
# `docker stop` the cell's container.
launch_cell() {
  local cg="$1" cell_id="$2" cell_dir="$3" container_name="$4" profile_id="$5"
  shift 5
  local lock_file
  lock_file="$(concurrency_group_lock_file "${cg}")"
  ensure_state_dirs

  local args_json
  args_json="$(printf '%s\n' "$@" | jq -R . | jq -s .)"

  local spec
  spec="$(jq -nc \
    --arg lf "${lock_file}" \
    --arg cg "${cg}" \
    --arg cid "${cell_id}" \
    --arg cdir "${cell_dir}" \
    --arg cn "${container_name}" \
    --arg p "${profile_id}" \
    --argjson args "${args_json}" \
    '{lock_file:$lf, concurrency_group:$cg, cell_id:$cid, cell_dir:$cdir,
      container_name:$cn, profile_id:$p, run_args:$args}')"

  # setsid puts the child in its own process group + session so we can
  # signal the whole tree via `kill -- -PID`.
  setsid "${SCRIPT_PATH}" __cell-runner <<<"${spec}" &
  local pid=$!
  CELL_PIDS+=("${pid}")
  CELL_BY_PID["${pid}"]="${cell_id}|${cell_dir}|${container_name}|${profile_id}"
}

# remove_pid removes a finished pid from the dispatch-loop tracking tables.
remove_pid() {
  local pid="$1"
  unset 'CELL_BY_PID['"${pid}"']'
  local new=() p
  for p in "${CELL_PIDS[@]}"; do
    [[ "${p}" == "${pid}" ]] || new+=("${p}")
  done
  CELL_PIDS=("${new[@]}")
}

# reap_one waits for one background cell to exit and removes it from tracking.
# Returns 0 if a child was reaped, 1 if there are none to wait on.
reap_one() {
  (( ${#CELL_PIDS[@]} > 0 )) || return 1
  local finished_pid=""
  set +e
  wait -n -p finished_pid "${CELL_PIDS[@]}"
  local rc=$?
  set -e
  if [[ -z "${finished_pid}" ]]; then
    # bash returned without a PID (e.g. signal). Reap any whose pids are dead.
    local p reaped=0
    for p in "${CELL_PIDS[@]}"; do
      if ! kill -0 "${p}" 2>/dev/null; then
        remove_pid "${p}"
        reaped=1
      fi
    done
    (( reaped )) && return 0
    return "${rc}"
  fi
  remove_pid "${finished_pid}"
  return 0
}

# on_signal sets the interrupt flag. Heavy shutdown work runs from the main
# dispatch loop after `wait` returns; trap handlers should be quick.
on_signal() {
  RUNNER_INTERRUPTED=1
}

# shutdown_in_flight SIGTERMs each in-flight cell's process group, `docker
# stop`s each in-flight container (if docker is available), waits up to
# BENCH_SHUTDOWN_GRACE_SECONDS, then SIGKILLs stragglers. Finally writes a
# final_status=interrupted report into every in-flight cell directory.
shutdown_in_flight() {
  local pids=("${CELL_PIDS[@]}")
  (( ${#pids[@]} > 0 )) || return 0

  warn "interrupt received: stopping ${#pids[@]} in-flight cell(s)"

  local has_docker=0
  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 && has_docker=1

  local pid info cn
  for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do
    kill -TERM -- "-${pid}" 2>/dev/null || true
    info="${CELL_BY_PID[${pid}]:-}"
    cn="${info#*|}"; cn="${cn#*|}"; cn="${cn%%|*}"
    if (( has_docker )) && [[ -n "${cn}" ]]; then
      docker stop --time "${BENCH_SHUTDOWN_GRACE_SECONDS}" "${cn}" \
        >/dev/null 2>&1 &
    fi
  done

  local deadline=$((SECONDS + BENCH_SHUTDOWN_GRACE_SECONDS))
  local any_alive=1
  while (( SECONDS < deadline )) && (( any_alive )); do
    any_alive=0
    for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do
      kill -0 "${pid}" 2>/dev/null && { any_alive=1; break; }
    done
    (( any_alive )) || break
    sleep 1
  done

  for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do
    if kill -0 "${pid}" 2>/dev/null; then
      kill -KILL -- "-${pid}" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
  done

  # Best-effort reap so shells don't leave zombies behind.
  for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do
    wait "${pid}" 2>/dev/null || true
  done

  local cell_id cell_dir profile_id rest
  for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do
    info="${CELL_BY_PID[${pid}]:-}"
    [[ -n "${info}" ]] || continue
    cell_id="${info%%|*}"
    rest="${info#*|}"
    cell_dir="${rest%%|*}"
    rest="${rest#*|}"
    # cn already extracted; skip to profile_id (4th field)
    rest="${rest#*|}"
    profile_id="${rest}"
    write_interrupted_report "${cell_dir}" "${cell_id}" "${profile_id}"
    inflight_remove "${cell_id}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  done
  CELL_PIDS=()
  CELL_BY_PID=()
}

# dispatch_cell_via_pool blocks until a job slot frees, then launches a cell
# via setsid + flock. Honors RUNNER_INTERRUPTED — returns 1 once the runner
# has been signalled so callers can break out of their own loops.
dispatch_cell_via_pool() {
  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3" framework="$4"
  local dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7" rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9"
  local adapter="${10}" attempt_of="${11}" te_ver="${12}" hd="${13}"

  (( RUNNER_INTERRUPTED )) && return 1

  while (( ${#CELL_PIDS[@]} >= JOBS )); do
    if (( RUNNER_INTERRUPTED )); then return 1; fi
    reap_one || break
  done
  (( RUNNER_INTERRUPTED )) && return 1

  local cell_id cell_dir cg cn
  cell_id="$(new_cell_id)"
  cell_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}/${cell_id}"
  cg="$(profile_concurrency_group "${profile_file}")"
  cn="fizeau-cell-${cell_id}"

  launch_cell "${cg}" "${cell_id}" "${cell_dir}" "${cn}" "${profile_id}" \
    "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" "${framework}" \
    "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}" \
    "${adapter}" "${attempt_of}" "${te_ver}" "${hd}"
}

# drain_cells reaps remaining background cells. Stops early when interrupted.
drain_cells() {
  while (( ${#CELL_PIDS[@]} > 0 )); do
    if (( RUNNER_INTERRUPTED )); then return 0; fi
    reap_one || break
  done
}

# run_sweep walks the (profile, bench-set, task, rep) matrix, applies resume /
# retry rules, and dispatches one cell per slot. Each cell is forked under
# setsid and holds the per-concurrency-group flock for its lifetime so:
#   - cells in the same concurrency-group serialize across local --jobs > 1
#     parallelism AND across concurrent ./benchmark invocations;
#   - SIGINT/SIGTERM to the runner cleanly reaches every in-flight cell.
run_sweep() {
  local profiles_csv="$1" bench_sets_csv="$2" out="$3"
  local force_rerun="$4" retry_invalid="$5" reps_override="$6"

  require_tool yq
  require_tool jq
  require_tool sha256sum
  require_tool flock
  require_tool setsid

  mkdir -p "${out}"
  ensure_state_dirs

  local te_ver hd
  te_ver="$(task_executor_version)"
  hd="$(harbor_runner_image_digest)"
  write_sweep_metadata "${out}" "${te_ver}" "${hd}"

  # Install signal handlers BEFORE dispatching any cells so an immediate
  # SIGTERM during startup is still observed by the dispatch loop.
  trap on_signal INT TERM

  IFS=',' read -r -a profile_ids <<<"${profiles_csv}"
  IFS=',' read -r -a bench_set_ids <<<"${bench_sets_csv}"

  local profile_id bs_id bs_file profile_file
  local framework dataset dataset_dir reps tasks harness adapter
  local terminal_file invalid_file orphan_file

  for profile_id in "${profile_ids[@]}"; do
    (( RUNNER_INTERRUPTED )) && break
    [[ -n "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
    profile_file="$(resolve_profile_file "${profile_id}")"
    local profile_json_full harness
    profile_json_full="$(yq -o=json '.' "${profile_file}")"
    harness="$(jq -r '.harness // "none"' <<<"${profile_json_full}")"
    adapter="$(resolve_adapter_for_harness "${harness}")"

    for bs_id in "${bench_set_ids[@]}"; do
      (( RUNNER_INTERRUPTED )) && break
      [[ -n "${bs_id}" ]] || continue
      bs_file="$(resolve_bench_set_file "${bs_id}")"
      framework="$(bench_set_framework "${bs_file}")"
      dataset="$(bench_set_dataset "${bs_file}")"
      dataset_dir="${dataset}"
      if [[ -n "${reps_override}" ]]; then
        reps="${reps_override}"
      else
        reps="$(bench_set_reps "${bs_file}")"
      fi
      tasks="$(resolve_tasks "${bs_file}")"

      local task
      while IFS= read -r task; do
        (( RUNNER_INTERRUPTED )) && break
        [[ -n "${task}" ]] || continue
        [[ "${task}" != "*all*" ]] || die "bench-set ${bs_id}: tasks: all is not supported by run path yet"

        terminal_file="$(mktemp)"; invalid_file="$(mktemp)"; orphan_file="$(mktemp)"
        inspect_existing_cells "${out}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" "${profile_id}" \
          "${terminal_file}" "${invalid_file}" "${orphan_file}"

        local terminal_count invalid_count orphan_count
        terminal_count="$(wc -l <"${terminal_file}" | tr -d ' ')"
        invalid_count="$(wc -l <"${invalid_file}" | tr -d ' ')"
        orphan_count="$(wc -l <"${orphan_file}" | tr -d ' ')"

        if [[ ${force_rerun} -eq 1 ]]; then
          local r
          for ((r = 1; r <= reps; r++)); do
            (( RUNNER_INTERRUPTED )) && break
            dispatch_cell_via_pool "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
              "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
              "${r}" "${reps}" "${adapter}" "" "${te_ver}" "${hd}" || break
          done
        elif [[ ${retry_invalid} -eq 1 ]]; then
          local prior
          while IFS= read -r prior; do
            (( RUNNER_INTERRUPTED )) && break
            [[ -n "${prior}" ]] || continue
            dispatch_cell_via_pool "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
              "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
              "1" "${reps}" "${adapter}" "${prior}" "${te_ver}" "${hd}" || break
          done <"${invalid_file}"
          while IFS= read -r prior; do
            (( RUNNER_INTERRUPTED )) && break
            [[ -n "${prior}" ]] || continue
            dispatch_cell_via_pool "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
              "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
              "1" "${reps}" "${adapter}" "${prior}" "${te_ver}" "${hd}" || break
          done <"${orphan_file}"
          warn "retry-invalid: profile=${profile_id} task=${task} reran invalid=${invalid_count} orphan=${orphan_count}"
        else
          if [[ ${terminal_count} -ge ${reps} ]]; then
            warn "skip: profile=${profile_id} task=${task} (terminal_cells=${terminal_count} >= reps=${reps})"
          else
            local need r idx
            need=$((reps - terminal_count))
            idx=$((terminal_count + 1))
            for ((r = 1; r <= need; r++)); do
              (( RUNNER_INTERRUPTED )) && break
              dispatch_cell_via_pool "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
                "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
                "${idx}" "${reps}" "${adapter}" "" "${te_ver}" "${hd}" || break
              idx=$((idx + 1))
            done
          fi
        fi

        rm -f "${terminal_file}" "${invalid_file}" "${orphan_file}"
      done <<<"${tasks}"
    done
  done

  drain_cells

  if (( RUNNER_INTERRUPTED )); then
    shutdown_in_flight
    return 130
  fi
  return 0
}
