#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Integration tests for the ./benchmark concurrency-group flock,
# SIGTERM/SIGINT handling, and in-flight.json state file (bead fizeau-86f89221).
#
# Exercises the three ACs of the bead description:
#
#   AC1. SIGTERM mid-sweep -> in-flight cell's report.json gets
#        final_status=interrupted (process_outcome=killed) and the runner
#        exits non-zero.
#   AC2. Two concurrent ./benchmark invocations in the same concurrency-group
#        serialize via flock -> no two cells run with overlapping wall-clock.
#   AC3. in-flight.json reflects the current concurrent-cell count, and stale
#        entries (PID no longer alive) are pruned on the next read.
#
# Uses BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE to swap in stub executors so docker is
# not required; HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN is unused here (we need
# scriptable timing for AC1/AC2).

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
BENCHMARK="${SCRIPT_DIR}/benchmark"

TMP_ROOT=""
cleanup() {
  if [[ -n "${TMP_ROOT}" && -d "${TMP_ROOT}" ]]; then
    # Best-effort kill any stragglers spawned under TMP_ROOT.
    pkill -KILL -f "BENCH_HOSTNAME_OVERRIDE=fizeau-test-${TEST_ID}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    rm -rf "${TMP_ROOT}"
  fi
}
trap cleanup EXIT

fail() {
  echo "FAIL: $*" >&2
  exit 1
}

require() {
  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1 || fail "required tool not found: $1"
}

require jq
require yq
require flock
require setsid
require sha256sum

TEST_ID="$$-$(date +%s)"
TMP_ROOT="$(mktemp -d)"
TASKS_DIR="${TMP_ROOT}/tasks"
STUB_DIR="${TMP_ROOT}/stubs"
STATE_DIR="${TMP_ROOT}/bench-state"
mkdir -p "${TASKS_DIR}" "${STUB_DIR}" "${STATE_DIR}"

PROFILE="sindri-lucebox"
BENCH_SET="tb-2-1-canary"
DATASET="terminal-bench-2-1"
HOST_ID="fizeau-test-${TEST_ID}"

env_for_runner() {
  echo "FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR=${STATE_DIR}"
  echo "BENCH_HOSTNAME_OVERRIDE=${HOST_ID}"
  echo "BENCH_TASKS_DIR=${TASKS_DIR}"
  echo "BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE=sha256:test-digest"
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# AC1: SIGTERM mid-sweep marks in-flight cells final_status=interrupted.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

echo "==> AC1: SIGTERM mid-sweep marks in-flight cells final_status=interrupted"

# Stub executor that sleeps a long time, simulating a cell still in-flight
# when SIGTERM arrives. A trap on SIGTERM exits 143 so the cell process
# group teardown is fast.
cat >"${STUB_DIR}/sleeping" <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
spec="$(cat)"
cell_dir="$(jq -r '.cell_dir' <<<"$spec")"
mkdir -p "$cell_dir"
trap 'echo "stub: SIGTERM" >&2; exit 143' TERM INT
sleep 60 &
wait
EOF
chmod +x "${STUB_DIR}/sleeping"

OUT1="${TMP_ROOT}/out-ac1"
mkdir -p "${OUT1}"

env \
  FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR="${STATE_DIR}" \
  BENCH_HOSTNAME_OVERRIDE="${HOST_ID}" \
  BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
  BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE="${STUB_DIR}/sleeping" \
  BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest" \
  BENCH_SHUTDOWN_GRACE_SECONDS=3 \
  "${BENCHMARK}" \
    --profile "${PROFILE}" \
    --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${OUT1}" \
  >"${TMP_ROOT}/ac1.log" 2>&1 &

runner_pid=$!

# Wait until a cell-state.json appears (the runner has begun a cell).
deadline=$((SECONDS + 15))
while (( SECONDS < deadline )); do
  if find "${OUT1}/cells" -name 'cell-state.json' -print -quit 2>/dev/null | grep -q .; then
    break
  fi
  sleep 0.2
done

if ! find "${OUT1}/cells" -name 'cell-state.json' -print -quit 2>/dev/null | grep -q .; then
  cat "${TMP_ROOT}/ac1.log" >&2 || true
  fail "AC1: no cell-state.json appeared after 15s; runner never started a cell"
fi

# Send SIGTERM and wait for the runner to fully exit.
kill -TERM "${runner_pid}" 2>/dev/null || true
set +e
wait "${runner_pid}"
runner_rc=$?
set -e

[[ "${runner_rc}" -ne 0 ]] \
  || fail "AC1: runner exited 0; expected non-zero on SIGTERM (log: ${TMP_ROOT}/ac1.log)"

# Locate the first cell and verify final_status=interrupted.
first_cell="$(find "${OUT1}/cells/${DATASET}" -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 2 -type d | head -n1)"
[[ -n "${first_cell}" ]] || fail "AC1: no cell directory found"
report="${first_cell}/report.json"
[[ -f "${report}" ]] || fail "AC1: no report.json at ${report}"

status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' "${report}")"
outcome="$(jq -r '.process_outcome // ""' "${report}")"
[[ "${status}" == "interrupted" ]] \
  || fail "AC1: expected final_status=interrupted, got '${status}' (report: ${report})"
[[ "${outcome}" == "killed" ]] \
  || fail "AC1: expected process_outcome=killed, got '${outcome}'"

# in-flight.json should be empty after shutdown.
if [[ -f "${STATE_DIR}/${HOST_ID}/in-flight.json" ]]; then
  remaining="$(jq -r '(.cells // []) | length' "${STATE_DIR}/${HOST_ID}/in-flight.json")"
  [[ "${remaining}" == "0" ]] \
    || fail "AC1: in-flight.json still has ${remaining} cell(s) after shutdown"
fi

echo "   PASS AC1 (rc=${runner_rc}, status=interrupted)"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# AC2: Two concurrent ./benchmark invocations in the same concurrency-group
#      serialize via flock; no overlapping cells.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

echo "==> AC2: same concurrency-group serializes across two concurrent runners"

# Stub executor that records its start + end timestamps to a shared log so we
# can assert no two cell intervals overlap.
TIMING_LOG="${TMP_ROOT}/timing.log"
: >"${TIMING_LOG}"
cat >"${STUB_DIR}/timing" <<EOF
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
spec="\$(cat)"
cell_dir="\$(jq -r '.cell_dir' <<<"\$spec")"
mkdir -p "\$cell_dir"
start_ns="\$(date +%s%N)"
sleep 1.5
end_ns="\$(date +%s%N)"
printf '%s\t%s\t%s\n' "\$start_ns" "\$end_ns" "\$cell_dir" >> "${TIMING_LOG}"
jq -n --arg t "\$(jq -r '.task_id' <<<"\$spec")" \\
  '{task_id:\$t, final_status:"completed", status:"completed"}' \\
  > "\$cell_dir/result.json"
EOF
chmod +x "${STUB_DIR}/timing"

OUT2A="${TMP_ROOT}/out-ac2a"
OUT2B="${TMP_ROOT}/out-ac2b"
mkdir -p "${OUT2A}" "${OUT2B}"

run_concurrent() {
  local out="$1"
  env \
    FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR="${STATE_DIR}" \
    BENCH_HOSTNAME_OVERRIDE="${HOST_ID}" \
    BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
    BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE="${STUB_DIR}/timing" \
    BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest" \
    "${BENCHMARK}" \
      --profile "${PROFILE}" \
      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
      --reps 1 \
      --out "${out}" \
    >"${out}.log" 2>&1
}

run_concurrent "${OUT2A}" &
pid_a=$!
run_concurrent "${OUT2B}" &
pid_b=$!

wait "${pid_a}" || fail "AC2: runner A failed; see ${OUT2A}.log"
wait "${pid_b}" || fail "AC2: runner B failed; see ${OUT2B}.log"

# Verify no overlap between any two intervals in TIMING_LOG.
python3 - "${TIMING_LOG}" <<'PY'
import sys, pathlib
log = pathlib.Path(sys.argv[1])
intervals = []
for line in log.read_text().splitlines():
    parts = line.split('\t')
    if len(parts) < 2: continue
    s, e = int(parts[0]), int(parts[1])
    intervals.append((s, e, parts[2] if len(parts) > 2 else ''))
intervals.sort()
overlaps = []
for i in range(1, len(intervals)):
    prev_s, prev_e, prev_c = intervals[i-1]
    cur_s, cur_e, cur_c = intervals[i]
    if cur_s < prev_e:
        overlaps.append((prev_c, cur_c, prev_e - cur_s))
if overlaps:
    for prev_c, cur_c, ns in overlaps:
        sys.stderr.write(f"OVERLAP {ns}ns: prev={prev_c} cur={cur_c}\n")
    sys.exit(1)
print(f"  {len(intervals)} intervals, none overlap")
PY

echo "   PASS AC2"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# AC3: in-flight.json reflects current cell count; stale entries pruned on
#      next read.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

echo "==> AC3: in-flight.json count + stale-PID pruning"

# Step 3a: pre-seed in-flight.json with a stale entry (dead PID).
STATE_HOST_DIR="${STATE_DIR}/${HOST_ID}"
mkdir -p "${STATE_HOST_DIR}"
STALE_PID=99999
# Make sure that PID is actually dead. If by miracle it's alive, pick another.
while kill -0 "${STALE_PID}" 2>/dev/null; do
  STALE_PID=$((STALE_PID + 1))
done

jq -n --argjson pid "${STALE_PID}" \
  '{cells: [{cell_id:"stale-cell-id", cell_dir:"/dev/null", container_name:"x",
             pid:$pid, pgid:$pid, profile:"x", concurrency_group:"x",
             started_at:"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}' \
  >"${STATE_HOST_DIR}/in-flight.json"

# Stub executor that lets the parent observe live in-flight.json mid-cell.
# It records the current in-flight count to a probe file BEFORE finishing.
COUNT_PROBE="${TMP_ROOT}/inflight-count.txt"
: >"${COUNT_PROBE}"
cat >"${STUB_DIR}/probe" <<EOF
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
spec="\$(cat)"
cell_dir="\$(jq -r '.cell_dir' <<<"\$spec")"
mkdir -p "\$cell_dir"
# Read in-flight.json under its lock so we see a consistent view.
(
  flock 9
  jq -r '(.cells // []) | length' "${STATE_HOST_DIR}/in-flight.json"
) 9>"${STATE_HOST_DIR}/in-flight.lock" >> "${COUNT_PROBE}"
jq -n --arg t "\$(jq -r '.task_id' <<<"\$spec")" \\
  '{task_id:\$t, final_status:"completed", status:"completed"}' \\
  > "\$cell_dir/result.json"
EOF
chmod +x "${STUB_DIR}/probe"

OUT3="${TMP_ROOT}/out-ac3"
mkdir -p "${OUT3}"

env \
  FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR="${STATE_DIR}" \
  BENCH_HOSTNAME_OVERRIDE="${HOST_ID}" \
  BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
  BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE="${STUB_DIR}/probe" \
  BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest" \
  "${BENCHMARK}" \
    --profile "${PROFILE}" \
    --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
    --reps 1 \
    --out "${OUT3}" \
  >"${TMP_ROOT}/ac3.log" 2>&1 \
    || fail "AC3: runner failed; log: ${TMP_ROOT}/ac3.log"

# Verify each cell saw a live count of 1 (itself; stale entry was pruned).
mapfile -t observed <"${COUNT_PROBE}"
[[ ${#observed[@]} -ge 1 ]] || fail "AC3: probe recorded no in-flight counts"
for n in "${observed[@]}"; do
  [[ "${n}" == "1" ]] \
    || fail "AC3: expected in-flight count=1 while a cell runs, observed=${n}"
done

# Verify in-flight.json is empty after the sweep completes.
final_count="$(jq -r '(.cells // []) | length' "${STATE_HOST_DIR}/in-flight.json")"
[[ "${final_count}" == "0" ]] \
  || fail "AC3: in-flight.json still has ${final_count} entries after sweep finished"

# Verify the seeded stale entry is gone.
stale_present="$(jq -r --arg cid "stale-cell-id" \
  '((.cells // []) | map(select(.cell_id == $cid)) | length)' \
  "${STATE_HOST_DIR}/in-flight.json")"
[[ "${stale_present}" == "0" ]] \
  || fail "AC3: stale entry (cell_id=stale-cell-id, pid=${STALE_PID}) was not pruned"

# Verify in-flight.json lives under the hostname namespace as documented.
[[ -f "${STATE_HOST_DIR}/in-flight.json" ]] \
  || fail "AC3: in-flight.json missing under <state-dir>/<hostname>"

echo "   PASS AC3 (live count=1 each cell, stale entry pruned)"

echo "PASS: AC1 + AC2 + AC3 satisfied"

reap_one() {
  (( ${#CELL_PIDS[@]} > 0 )) || return 1
  # `wait -n -p VAR` leaves VAR unset (not just empty) when interrupted, which
  # would trip `set -u` below.
  local finished_pid=""
  set +e
  set +u
  wait -n -p finished_pid "${CELL_PIDS[@]}"
  local rc=$?
  set -u
  set -e

  if [[ -z "${finished_pid}" ]]; then
    # bash returned without a PID (e.g. signal). Reap any whose pids are dead.
    local p reaped=0
    for p in "${CELL_PIDS[@]}"; do

        reaped=1
      fi
    done

feat(bench): concurrency-group flock + signal handling + in-flight.json

Wraps each cell in scripts/benchmark/benchmark in a setsid'd child that
acquires the per-concurrency-group flock for its lifetime, enabling both
local --jobs N parallelism and cross-process serialization for cells
that share a backend lane. Adds SIGINT/SIGTERM trap that SIGTERMs each
in-flight cell's process group, `docker stop`s the corresponding
harbor-runner container, waits BENCH_SHUTDOWN_GRACE_SECONDS, then
SIGKILLs stragglers, and stamps final_status=interrupted /
process_outcome=killed onto any cell that was still running. Tracks
concurrent cells in ${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}/<hostname>/in-flight.json,
pruning stale (dead-PID) entries on the next read. Harbor task-executor
honors a container_name field in task-spec so the runner can address
each container deterministically.

Satisfies parent fizeau-11e9c095 AC5. [fizeau-86f89221]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
